mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 2 Series M235i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M235i (N55) engine typically costs £4,000–£8,500 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£2,000 depending on workshop location. Main dealer new engines can exceed £9,000 supply only. Used engines typically range from £2,500–£4,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 2 Series M235i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N55 is one of BMW's more reliable modern engines. It's far more robust than the twin-turbo N54 it replaced and doesn't suffer the timing chain issues of BMW's four-cylinder engines. However, it has known weak points: the plastic charge pipe is prone to cracking, wastegate rattle is common, and gaskets leak with age. With proper maintenance, these engines can exceed 150,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 2 Series M235i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Charge pipe failure (factory plastic unit cracks under boost), wastegate rattle (P30FF code, rattling on cold start and deceleration), oil leaks from valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets, and electric water pump failure (especially over 80,000 miles). Timing chain issues are less common on the N55 than on earlier BMW engines.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW M235i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold: if your M235i is worth £15,000 and a recon engine is £6,000 (40%), it's worth it. If the vehicle is a tired £8,000 example, the same engine exceeds 70% — get a scrap valuation first. The M235i's strong enthusiast following and retained value often justify replacement spend.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 2 Series M235i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The M235i (2014–2016) uses the N55B30O0 — a 3.0-litre turbocharged inline-six producing 322–326 bhp. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the charge pipe, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The factory plastic charge pipe can be replaced with an OEM unit (£150–£400), but many owners upgrade to an aftermarket aluminium charge pipe (£300–£600) as a preventative measure. The aluminium unit will not crack under boost and is a worthwhile investment for any N55-powered car.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW 2 Series M235i Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the only engine generation this badge ever used — the N55B30O0. See the Era Map above for era-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an M235i engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The N55 is a well-understood engine, but access can be tight in the 2 Series engine bay.

cta.label: → Compare M235i engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Charge Pipe Failure (N55)
problems.symptoms: Sudden loss of power, drivetrain malfunction warning, boost leak, check engine light, audible pop or hiss from the engine bay.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — the factory plastic charge pipe is a known weak point and can fail at any time.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, the car will run in limp mode with significantly reduced power.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The factory plastic charge pipe is notorious for cracking under boost pressure. Many owners upgrade to an aftermarket aluminium charge pipe as a preventative measure. If you experience a sudden loss of power or a drivetrain malfunction warning, this is one of the first things to check.

problems.title: Wastegate Rattle (Turbocharger)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine bay on cold start, rattling upon deceleration, loss of power, check engine light (P30FF), poor fuel economy.
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles — can appear earlier with aggressive driving.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; severe wastegate wear may require full turbo replacement.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: Wastegate rattle is a common issue on the N55, particularly on earlier models. The wastegate bushing wears over time, causing excessive movement and rattling. BMW has replaced many turbocharger assemblies under warranty to correct this issue. If you hear a rattle on cold start or deceleration, have the turbocharger inspected. Adjustment of the wastegate actuator can sometimes solve the issue, but the problem may return.

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Valve Cover & Oil Filter Housing Gaskets)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell, visible oil on the engine or garage floor, low oil level warnings, smoke from the engine bay.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — age-related as gaskets harden and deteriorate.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, oil leaks can lead to oil starvation or damage to other components.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The N55 is prone to oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket. These are common wear items as the engine ages. If you notice a burning oil smell or visible oil, have the gaskets inspected promptly. The valve cover itself can also warp and may need replacement, not just the gasket.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Simple fix — upgrade to aluminium for peace of mind
Adjustment or rebuild cheaper than full turbo replacement
Common maintenance item — repair when symptoms appear
Preventative — replace before oil loss causes damage
Full engine replacement required if bottom-end or valvetrain fails

buyingChecks.title: Buying This M235i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
* Charge pipe replaced with an aftermarket aluminium unit
* No wastegate rattle on cold start or deceleration
* Water pump and thermostat replaced (especially over 80,000 miles)
avoidIf: * Plastic charge pipe still fitted — it will fail eventually
* Wastegate rattle or P30FF code present
* Evidence of oil leaks from valve cover or oil filter housing
* Poor service history — the N55 is sensitive to oil quality

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The N55-powered M235i is one of the sweet spots in BMW's modern lineup — a reliable, tuneable, and characterful inline-six. It's far more robust than the N54 it replaced and doesn't suffer the timing chain nightmares of BMW's four-cylinders. However, it has its weak points: the charge pipe will fail, wastegate rattle is common, and gaskets will leak with age. A £6,000 reconditioned engine on an M235i valued at £15,000 is 40% of the car's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. With the 70% BMW scrap threshold, the M235i's strong retained value makes engine replacement a viable option.

cta.label: → Compare M235i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 2 Series M235i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (wastegate rattle is common on the N55)
* Charge Pipe — Check if the factory plastic charge pipe has been replaced with aluminium
* Service History — Regular oil changes every 8,000–10,000 miles using BMW-approved oil
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil around the valve cover and oil filter housing
* Smoke Check — Blue smoke on startup could indicate turbo seal wear
* Water Pump — Ask if the electric water pump and thermostat have been replaced (especially over 80,000 miles)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any P30FF (wastegate) codes?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
